chunks.text: 2025-26 Special Events - EECS at Berkeley. Professor Andy Neureuther (center) and former students. Celebration of Andy Neureuther. Join us for a special event celebrating Andy Neureuther’s influential career in optical lithography and semiconductor technology. Andy will share stories and memories from his decades of groundbreaking work and lasting impact on the community. See event photos.
chunks.question: What is being celebrated about Andy Neureuther?
chunks.answer: His career in optical lithography and semiconductor technology.

chunks.text: Recipients of the EECS Outstanding TA Award and the EECS Chairs. Student Awards 2025. Each year the EECS Student Awards Committee selects winners for each of our department awards, many based on nominations gathered from EECS students, faculty and staff. See the Winners.
chunks.question: How are EECS department award winners selected?
chunks.answer: By the Student Awards Committee based on nominations.

chunks.text: EECS Event Calendar. About. Diversity. Undergraduate Diversity Programs. $1M Hopper-Dean Foundation Gift for Diversity in CS. Presidents of Women in Computer Science and Engineering (WICSE). Diversity Milestones.
chunks.question: What supports diversity in computer science at EECS?
chunks.answer: A $1M Hopper-Dean Foundation gift and programs.

chunks.text: Visiting EECS. By the Numbers. History. Joseph Thomas Gier. The First Women of EECS. A Relatively Recent History: Women Doctoral Graduates in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences, 1969-1981.
chunks.question: What historical aspects are covered in EECS history?
chunks.answer: Joseph Thomas Gier and early women graduates.
